title: List all heterozygous variants
description: Identify every variant in the VCF whose sample genotype is heterozygous (0/1 or 1/0). Return them as a set of CHROM:POS:REF:ALT strings. Tests genotype-field parsing and structured set output.
task_prompt: You are given a VCF file at sample.vcf in your working directory. For each variant record, the sample column (SAMPLE1, the last column) contains the genotype as the first colon-separated subfield (the GT field per the FORMAT column). Identify every variant where the genotype is heterozygous - exactly '0/1' or '1/0'. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with the schema: {"het_variants": ["<CHROM>:<POS>:<REF>:<ALT>", ...]}. Use the literal CHROM string (e.g. 'chr1'), the POS as a string of digits, and the REF/ALT as single-letter strings. Order does not matter.
notes: 6 of 10 variants are 0/1 (het); the other 4 are 1/1 (hom alt). No 0/0 or 1/0 cases in this fixture.
